underlying: AAA national average regular gas price ($/gal) on Apr 30, 2026
method: Normal CDF P(AAA_4/30 > s) = 1 - Phi((s-mu)/sigma) with mu=4.275, sigma=0.022, clipped to [0.005, 0.995]. AUDIT-RECALIBRATED 2026-04-29 from prior mu=4.215 sigma=0.030. Resolution criterion confirmed three ways via audit: (1) every market's rules_primary reads 'strictly greater than $X on Apr 30, 2026 according to AAA' (identical wording to daily KXAAAGASD series); (2) prior month KXAAAGASM-26MAR31 settled at expiration_value=4.018 matching the AAA single-day Mar 31 print, NOT a March monthly average; (3) strike_date is the named day at 14:00Z. The 'M' suffix is just Kalshi's naming for the monthly event whose terminal market is the last-day daily print -- no monthly averaging anywhere. Anchor logic: market-implied median ~$4.275 (extrapolation of Apr 29 anchor 4.245 + ~3c additional 1-day drift). Sigma 0.022 from the 4.250-4.300 quote curve. Right-skew note retained: sigma should not go below 0.020 given Hormuz/Druzhba tail.
notes: AUDIT FIX 2026-04-29: mu 4.215->4.275, sigma 0.030->0.022. Resolution mechanism confirmed single-day Apr 30 AAA print (NOT a monthly average) -- prior month KXAAAGASM-26MAR31 settled at expiration_value=4.018 = AAA single-day. Anchor extrapolates Apr 29 theo (4.245) + 1 day of continued drift = 4.275. Reproduces market: 4.250 model 0.87, 4.270 model 0.59, 4.290 model 0.25 -- all within a couple cents of market across the curve. The 50-70c 'edge' from prior theo was model error, not real edge. Right-skew note: sigma should not go below 0.020 given Hormuz/Druzhba tail.
